commit.message:
test: add 126 schema/IPC validation tests (#124)

* test: add 126 schema/IPC validation tests

Add comprehensive test coverage for schemas and IPC wire format:

schema_validation_tests.rs (66 tests):
- Serialize/deserialize roundtrips for all schema types (Device,
  Profile, FilterConfig, TelemetryData, NormalizedTelemetry,
  TelemetryFlags, TelemetryValue, CalibrationData, LedConfig,
  HapticsConfig, ProfileScope, DeviceState, DeviceType, CalibrationType)
- Schema evolution tests (unknown fields, missing optional fields,
  version detection, future schema rejection)
- Cross-format tests (JSON <-> protobuf for Device, TelemetryData,
  Profile, DeviceCapabilities, FilterConfig)
- Default value handling and stability for all config types
- Profile JSON schema validation (valid/invalid/malformed)
- IPC conversion validation (invalid device type, missing capabilities,
  out-of-range values, empty-string-to-None conversion)
- Domain validation edge cases (NaN, infinity, out-of-range)
- Proptest fuzzing (torque, gain, frequency, degrees, curve points,
  cnm roundtrip, telemetry values, telemetry data, device ID validation)

ipc_wire_format_validation_tests.rs (60 tests):
- Wire format stability (known byte layout, encode/decode identity,
  deterministic protobuf, max/zero field values)
- Message framing (multi-message streams, empty payloads, flag
  preservation, header-only parsing)
- Large message handling (zero-length rejection, oversized rejection,
  custom limits, encode/decode limit enforcement, encode_to_buffer)
- Concurrent message sequencing (monotonic sequences, interleaved
  types, u32::MAX wrap)
- Error message formatting (all variants have display, recoverable/
  fatal classification, helper constructors, version info in errors)
- Transport/config validation (TCP defaults, custom address, builder
  overrides, serde roundtrips, Windows named pipe)
- Version compatibility (same/higher/lower/malformed versions)
- Server lifecycle (start/stop, double-start rejection, feature
  negotiation compatible/incompatible/unsupported, health broadcast,
  client register/unregister, stop clears clients)
- Flag edge cases (response+error combo, streaming+compressed,
  idempotent set)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: rustfmt import ordering in schema validation tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: gracefully handle missing proxy credentials in BatchService (#175)

BatchService.__init__ crashed with ProxyConfigurationError when
PROXY_ENABLED=true but credentials were not set, causing 500 errors
on /api/v1/batches. This also broke CORS since the error propagated
past the CORS middleware without headers.

Now catches ProxyConfigurationError and sets _proxy_service to None,
matching the pattern used in video_service.py and youtube_service.py.

commit.message:
fix(clippy): resolve all workspace clippy warnings under -D warnings (#3318)

## Summary

Resolves **~200+ clippy warnings** across the entire workspace, making
`cargo clippy --workspace --no-default-features --features cpu -- -D
warnings` pass **cleanly**.

This unblocks the **Feature cpu** CI workflow which was red on every PR.

## Crates Fixed

| Crate | Before | After |
|-------|--------|-------|
| `bitnet-kernels` | 129 errors | 0 |
| `bitnet-gpu-hal` | 36 errors | 0 |
| `bitnet-fuzz` (20+ targets) | ~25 errors | 0 |
| `bitnet-api-versioning-core` | 3 errors | 0 |
| `xtask` | 2 errors | 0 |
| `bitnet-server` | 1 error | 0 |
| `bitnet-wgpu-runner` | 1 error | 0 |
| `bitnet-ffi` | 1 error | 0 |

## Categories Fixed

- **Safety docs** (40): Added `# Safety` sections to all unsafe NEON
functions
- **needless_range_loop** (~50): Replaced index loops with iterator
patterns
- **Manual stdlib reimplementations** (~25): `div_ceil`,
`is_multiple_of`, `clamp`
- **Collapsible if** (~15): Merged nested ifs in fuzz targets
- **Single-pattern match** (~12): Replaced with `if let`
- **Const fn / must_use / Default** (~5): Added missing attributes
- **Float precision / literals / casts** (~10): Fixed excessive
precision, added separators
- **Other** (~10): similar names, manual contains, assign operations

## Verification

```bash
# All clean (3 consecutive runs confirmed)
cargo clippy --locked --workspace --no-default-features --features cpu -- -D warnings
# Build passes
RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo check --workspace --no-default-features --features cpu
```

60 files changed, 556 insertions, 454 deletions.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf(inference): eliminate hidden buffer allocation in DenseFFN::forward (#3313)

## What changed
Reuse the \gate\ projection buffer for the \silu(gate)*up\ computation
instead of allocating a separate \hidden\ Vec via \collect()\.

This eliminates one \intermediate_dim\-sized allocation per FFN forward
call (one per layer per token). For a 2B-param model with
\intermediate_dim=8192\, this saves ~32KB per layer per token.

## Change
\\\diff
- let hidden: Vec<f32> = gate.iter().zip(up.iter()).map(|(g, u)| silu(g)
* u).collect();
- self.down_proj.forward(&hidden)
+ for (g, &u) in gate.iter_mut().zip(up.iter()) { *g = silu(*g) * u; }
+ self.down_proj.forward(&gate)
\\\

## Local verification
- \cargo test -p bitnet-inference --lib dense_forward
--no-default-features --features cpu\ 19/19 pass
- \cargo clippy -p bitnet-inference --no-default-features --features
cpu\ clean

## Receipt
- **What changed**: DenseFFN::forward in dense_forward.rs
- **Local**: cargo test 19/19 pass
- **CI**: Relying on CI Core (Build & Test, Clippy, Grid Check, Docs)
- **Parity**: No kernel changes; pure allocation optimization
- **Docs**: none
- **Risks**: None behavioral identity preserved (same computation, fewer
allocs)
- **Triage**: A (MERGE NOW)

-->

* **Performance**
* Optimized memory allocation in neural network inference computation
layers, reducing overhead while maintaining output accuracy.

commit.message:
Video support (#2048)

This PR adds support for handling video/video frames within the
experimental datumaro dataset class. It includes a new MediaField class
that can handle both a video frame or an image to allow datasets with
mixed media, several new converters such as video frame -> image
conversion. The import/export logic for datasets is also updated to
handle video files.

Video classes added: `VideoFramePathField`, `VideoFrameCallableField`,
`VideoInfo`, `LazyVideoFrame`, `VideoFrameCache`.
Video converters added: `VideoFramePathToImageConverter`,
`VideoFrameToImageCallableConverter`

Also added generic variants of the above classes such as
`MediaPathField` or `MediaInfo` that work with either video frames or
images. This allows Samples to have a single field that stores video
frames or images.

Export logic is updated to work with videos. Added an ExportMode enum
that is used for both images and videos which determines how files
should be handled in the export (SKIP, REFERENCE or COPY).

resolves #2042

* Fix CI: don't delete thirdparty dir before zip extract, preserving git-tracked headers

The andeon thirdparty-libs.zip only contains .lib/.dll files, not headers.
The zip also has a thirdparty/ prefix, so extracting with -othirdparty created
a nested thirdparty/thirdparty/ structure. Fix: extract at repo root so the
zip's thirdparty/ prefix maps correctly, and keep headers from git checkout.

The flare/sources/flare/CMakeLists.txt had no install(TARGETS ...) rules,
so `ninja install` only installed xdg-data metadata files. AppImage creation

for Flare binary, helper programs, shared libraries, and the stuff directory,

* Fix macOS build: rename toonzlib→flarelib in tfarm CMakeLists.txt

The add_dependencies call in flarefarm/tfarm/CMakeLists.txt still referenced
the old target name "toonzlib" which was renamed to "flarelib" during the
rebrand. This caused CMake configure to fail on macOS with:
  "The dependency target 'toonzlib' of target 'tfarm' does not exist."

Co-authored-by: charlieduzstuf <99685295+charlieduzstuf@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Windows CI: enable find_package(OpenCV) instead of blocking it

The previous code set CMAKE_DISABLE_FIND_PACKAGE_OpenCV=ON which
prevented find_package(OpenCV) from running, even when the workflow
passes -DOpenCV_DIR pointing to C:\tools\opencv\build. The vcpkg
fallback then failed (no vcpkg OpenCV installed), resulting in
OpenCV_FOUND=OFF and fatal 'opencv2/opencv.hpp' not found errors.

Fix: use find_package(OpenCV QUIET) which respects OpenCV_DIR and
falls back gracefully if not found.

Co-authored-by: charlieduzstuf <99685295+charlieduzstuf@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Windows CI: add retry logic for vcpkg bootstrap (transient network timeout)

The bootstrap-vcpkg.bat download of vcpkg.exe can fail with
WinHttpReceiveResponse timeout on GitHub runners. Add up to 3 retries
with 10s delays between attempts, matching the pattern used for
git clone retries in the same script.
